feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Strategy
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Strategy Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Open Connectors Adobe Commerce Magento 2.4.6 support
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I'm exploring integration options for Adobe Commerce (Magento Commerce) and until now have found only article/documentation describing Magento 2.0 available REST API integration on the following links:
Overview | Magento v2.0 | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

SAP Business Accelerator Hub

In case we would go with the Standard Edition and have Open Connector for Magento from API Hub in place, we noticed that Magento 2.0 is supported. However, we run on 2.4.6 version already.

I would like to be sure that there is full backward compatibility supported before proceeding with such project, or otherwise, to find the future release roadmap and make sure that the Adobe Commerce up to date releases will be supported.

Do you know if available REST API is fully supported for Adobe Commerce 2.4.6.?

Is there future release roadmap available?

Many thanks for the answers, upfront!

Kristian

entry.title: SOA Manager - Distributing Integration Scenarios - Multiple Domain Systems with one central system
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

This question is regarding SOA-Manager integration scenario distribution using one central S/4Hana system to other S/4Hana systems connected as a domain system.

For Ex. I have 5 S4Hana systems in my landscape like system A, system B, system C, system D, system E

Assuming system A is the central S/4Hana system for SOA-Manager,

Can I create integration scenarios between system B and system C and distribute it using central system A ?

Also, what could be the other possible use case and strategy for a central S/4Hana system for SOA-Manager?

Thank you in advance 🙂

entry.title: Response was of unexpected text/html ContentType. Incoming portion of HTML stream: (none) in cpi
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are replicating utilities contract account from SAP S4hana on-premise to SAP Cloud for Customer via sap cpi while in trace it showing error : Response was of unexpected text/html ContentType. Incoming portion of HTML stream: (none) .Here our flow of connection is IDOC to SOAP where IDOC is sender and soap is receiver adapter . Can anyone please suggest resolution to this error .

Thanks

entry.title: Redundant external calls during order creation via API_SALES_ORDER_SRV
entry.content:
Hello, we are seeing repetitive calls during order creation via API_SALES_ORDER_SRV to RFC_CALCULATE_TAXES_DOC and /DMA/VALIDATE_ADDRESS. When replicating the same order creating via manually entry within SAP UI this behavior is not the same (only 1 call made to each in this case).

Reaching out to this community to see if anyone has encountered similar behavior and if there was any remediation that was found.

Thank you in advanced for the time and ideas.

Tom Woelfel

entry.title: DefaultXIService - How do we change this
entry.content:
Dear All,

I've created an iFlow responsible for receiving ABAP proxy synchronous calls and converting them into SOAP receiver calls. However, when we execute the scenario from ERP, we've observed that the message monitor displays "DefaultXIService" in the sender section of the response as shown below. We are interested in changing this to a different name, but I couldn't locate a specific setting within the iFlow to do so. Any suggestions or ideas on how to achieve this?

entry.title: Getting 'Error in HTTP Access: IF_HTTP_CLIENT->RECEIVE 1' while testing content repository
entry.content:
HI Team,

I am new to SAP with limited knowledge in it. Here I am trying to create new Http Content Repository which we are planning to use as external storage so that when we run any archival using T-Code SARA then archived file should be placed in directory associated with this given Http Content Repository.

Following is the error screenshot I am getting.

Could you please help or guide us in this situation on how to fix this issue or any alternative to use to get this done.

Appreciating any help in advance.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - Message Metric Question on File Based Interfaces ?
entry.content:
Hello

This is a question regarding - How a message is defined and metered in SAP Integration Suite.

Scenario - File Based Integrations. Inbound and Outbound. Example below

  • Inbound - Upload a file from application server file (AL11) into SAP using SAP Program.
  • Outbound - Generate a file using SAP Program and save the file to a folder in application server

Question : File based Integrations in SAP counted as messages by SAP Integration Suite meters ?

entry.title: SAP CI JDBC Adapter SQL Query Log
entry.content:
Dear All,

We used to have logSQLStatement in SAP PI/PO to log the sql query that is being used by the JDBC adapter. Do we have something similar in CPI? I want to see query that is being executed by CPI on DB. I traced the iFlow but nothing found in them. Where can we find this info?

entry.title: Local Integration Process vs ProcessDirect
entry.content:
Dear All,

While reviewing the main iFlow, I identified a portion that could be separated into a reusable set of steps. This led me to consider two scenarios:

  1. Integrating those steps into a local integration process and invoking it repeatedly from the main iFlow.

  2. Creating a distinct ProcessDirect iFlow and invoking it from the main iFlow.

Since there's only one iFlow requiring this functionality, both options are viable. However, I'm seeking guidance on which option would be more efficient in terms of performance.

entry.title: Message Mapping Custom Function Debug
entry.content:
Dear All,

How to debug the custom groovy functions written using Package com.sap.it.api.mapping in IntelliJ IDEA?
I couldn't find any jar file for this package.

entry.title: Clarification regaridng exception subprocess
entry.content:
Dear All,

As you are aware, when we create an exception subprocess, CI suggests using a start event as the first step instead of an error start. I've noticed that both of them behave the same way in my scenario. Could you please clarify the differences between them and guide on when to use each?

entry.title: replicate only contacts with specific attributes from Sales Cloud to Emarsys
entry.content:
Hi,

we use Emarsys Account Engagement with Sales Cloud v1. Due to legal reasons only certain contacts may be replicated from Sales Cloud to Emarsys. Is it possible to filter the contacts being replicated (=prevent them from being replicated)?

Regards

Christian

entry.title: Integration SAP Sales C4C with SAP Emarsys
entry.content:
Hi,

according to here: https://emarsys.com/partner-ecosystem/directory/technology-partners/sap-sales-cloud/ it looks to me as if there is an out-of-the-box integration between SAP Sales C4C and Emarsys available (e.g. to replicate contacts from C4C to Emarsys). Unfortunately I cannot find any documentation about this. Can you help me with this please?

Regards

Christian

entry.title: Integrate B1 to third party WMS
entry.content:
Hello friends, i'm new to the Sap World. I need to create an Api to sync data between Sap B1 (hana) to third party WMS System (Winthor).

Is there an way to catch those data once the object is updated or inserted ? Like a Sql trigger? for example, I need to send all products inserts or update to the Wms.

Is the B1IF 2.0 the best way I can do this? Can some one give an scenario example ?

My external rest its an NodeJS rest api.

Another way I was thinking about, its to use just the service layer, where I could create a nodecron (like php cronjob) to send an Get request to Sap B1, every x seconds, returning entire list of products (for example) than In my code I would find a way to check if I need to send it to wms or not, but I dont think this second way is effective.

Thanks!

entry.title: Authentication of API
entry.content:
Dear All,

I exposed an end point through CI and also created a service instance with a key using client id and secret. But the API consumer is able to use both OAuth and basic authentication using the same client id. How to keep only oauth as the option and restrict the basic for this scenario

entry.title: CI Container Store Retrive Data in Message Mapping
entry.content:
Dear All,

We have Container object in PI/PO which helps to store some data for later retrieval in the same mapping. Is there any similar feature within mapping?

entry.title: Error Handling
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I'm currently dealing with the following scenario:

iFlow1 receives synchronous messages from a third party via the HTTP adapter. After performing mapping and conversions, it invokes another ProcessDirect, iFlow2, which in turn calls an OData service in ECC using the HTTP adapter. However, in instances where the OData service in ECC encounters an error, this error is not being propagated back to iFlow2 and subsequently to iFlow1. The monitoring output I observe is as follows:

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: HTTP operation failed invoking http://xyz.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZOData_SRV/entitySet with statusCode: 400. The MPL ID for the failed message is

But the actual OData error is something else. How to get this error in CPI?

I tried with Exception handling but it also brings above error message and not the error from OData.

entry.title: ProcessDirect Adapter and Decoupling
entry.content:
Dear All,

Within SAP CPI/CPI, the ProcessDirect adapter serves to modularize the entire iFlow. When both producer and consumer iFlows utilize this adapter, it implies they are effectively decoupled and operate with distinct Camel exchanges. Presently, I am faced with a scenario necessitating the decoupling of the Timer step from the primary flow, and I am contemplating employing the ProcessDirect adapter to invoke the main iFlow. While SAP recommends employing the SOAP adapter for such cases, it's worth noting that SOAP calls necessitate interaction with the LoadBalancer, unlike ProcessDirect.

I seek your advice on the optimal approach to decoupling both the Timer and main iFlows in accordance with best practices. Is SOAP the sole viable option in this scenario, or are there alternative methodologies to consider? Your insights would be greatly appreciated. Thanks.

Regards,
David

entry.title: Integrating Legacy SAP Systems with Third Party iPaaS Tools like Celigo
entry.content:
I am currently exploring integration strategies for legacy SAP systems, for example SAP ECC, with third party integration platforms like Celigo iPaaS. Our objective is to create seamless data flows between older SAP systems and other applications. I'm seeking insights and experiences from the community regarding best practices, challenges, and recommendations for such integrations. Has anyone worked on similar integration projects? What are the key considerations, particularly around compatibility and security? Any shared experiences or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Also if people are interested in piloting this, please contact me.

entry.title: Content Modifier XPATH IDOC
entry.content:
Dear All,

We have a IDoc XML being received through IDoc adapter and I tried to read the value of MESTYP using ContentModifier but the value is always coming as empty. I tried both header and property section like

MESTYP - //EDI_DC40/MESTYP (XPATH)

Any idea?

IDoc XML:

<FLOC01 xmlns="urn:sap-com:document:sap:idoc:soap:messages">
  <IDOC BEGIN="1">
    <EDI_DC40 SEGMENT="1">
      <TABNAM>EDI_DC40</TABNAM>
      <MANDT>XXX</MANDT>
      <DOCNUM>0000000000001278</DOCNUM>
      <DOCREL>XXX</DOCREL>
      <STATUS>XX</STATUS>
      <DIRECT>X</DIRECT>
      <OUTMOD>X</OUTMOD>
      <IDOCTYP>FLOCXX</IDOCTYP>
      <MESTYP>FLOCX</MESTYP>
      <STDMES>/EVISX</STDMES>
      <SNDPOR>SAPXXXX</SNDPOR>
      <SNDPRT>XX</SNDPRT>
      <SNDPRN>XXXXXXXXX</SNDPRN>
      <RCVPOR>XXXX_XXX</RCVPOR>
      <RCVPRT>XX</RCVPRT>
      <RCVPRN>XXXXXXXXX</RCVPRN>
      <CREDAT>YYYYMMDD</CREDAT>
      <CRETIM>HHMMSS</CRETIM>
      <ARCKEY>urn:sap.com:msgid=XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX</ARCKEY>
      <SERIAL>YYYYMMDDHHMMSS</SERIAL>
    </EDI_DC40>
    <FLOC_HDR SEGMENT="1">
      <ACTION>X</ACTION>
      <TPLNR>XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX</TPLNR>
      <STRNO>XX.XX.XXXX.XXXX.XX-XXX-XXX</STRNO>
      <TPLMA>XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX</TPLMA>
      <TPLMA_L>XX.XX.XXXX.XXXX</TPLMA_L>
      <PLTXT>ITEM DESCRIPTION</PLTXT>
      <EQART>XX-XXXX</EQART>
      <BUKRS>XXXX</BUKRS>
      <IWERK>XXXX</IWERK>
      <SWERK>XXXX</SWERK>
      <EQFNR>XX-XXX-XXX</EQFNR>
      <KOSTL>XXXXXXXXXX</KOSTL>
      <STORT>XX-XXX</STORT>
    </FLOC_HDR>
  </IDOC>
</FLOC01>
